How do we view our sin? Pastor, I have no sin. You've deceived yourselves. You're making God a liar, and His truth is not in you, according to 1 John 1, verse 8 and verse 10. How do we view our sin? How can I do this great wickedness and sin against God? You say, well, my sin isn't fornication, my sin isn't adultery. That's what Joseph would be, that's why he calls it great wickedness. Can I remind you that Revelation 21, verse number 8 says, all liars shall have their part in the lake which burns with fire and brimstone? Can I remind you that James says that if we offend in one, that we're guilty of all? The problem is that sometimes we've gotten comfortable with, can I call them little sins tonight? You fixed the hair, you got the thing out of your teeth because you had dinner that had herbs on it and you got stuff. You understand, right? We don't get mad at the mirror. That'd be silly. So when we look into the perfect law of liberty, the Holy Spirit of God convicts us. Don't cover it. Don't excuse it. Don't get mad. Get right. Get right.
